{{Quote|Well, the bounty hunter we ran into on Ord Mantell changed my mind.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|<ref name="Episode 5">{{Film|V}}</ref>}}
"'''The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell'''" is a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]], originally published in [[February]] [[1981]] by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]]. It was later reprinted in ''[[Amazing Heroes]]'' magazine #1–5.

It was republished by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[Classic Star Wars 1|''Classic Star Wars'' 1]], and up to page 15 of [[Classic Star Wars 2|''Classic Star Wars'' 2]]. It was republished again on [[StarWars.com]]'s webstrips section of [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] and was made available to the public after penciller Al Williamson passed away on June 14, 2010.

==Plot summary==
While searching for a new planet to host a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] base, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia's]] ship gets destroyed, and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] has to come rescue them. They escape, but Solo's ship suffers major damage. Han stops for repairs on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]].

When the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Skorr/Legends|Skorr]] sees that Han has landed on Ord Mantell, he hatches a plan to capture Han and cash in on [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure's]] [[Death mark/Legends|death mark]] against the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]. During the night, Skorr kidnaps Luke and Leia and tells Solo that he will free Luke and Leia if Han surrenders to the bounty hunter. As proof of the kidnapping Skorr gives Han [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|Luke's lightsaber]].

The next morning Han flies to the arranged meet-up place, [[Ten Mile Plateau]]. After Skorr frees Luke and Leia, the three are saved by [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. They make their way to the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and escape from the planet.
{{Plot}}

==Development==
The story is based on a line Han Solo uttered in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

The strip was the first ''Star Wars'' newspaper strip released in the ''Los Angeles Times'' since the Star Wars Fan Club lobbied for the series' continuation after its near cancellation.
